story: XPRIZE Wildfire 'Autonomous Wildfire Response' finals held in Alaska, Nenana Municipal Airport (near Fairbanks), June 15-28 2026; UAF ACUASI managed the airspace. Finalist Dryad Networks says it demonstrated autonomous detection + suppression there, no human in loop. Winner announced Sept 2026.
cleared_on_screen: $11 million XPRIZE Wildfire competition (per XPRIZE / Dryad)
Nearly 300 teams narrowed to 3 finalists in the Autonomous Wildfire Response track (per XPRIZE/Dryad)
Finalists: Anduril, AURA Foresight, Dryad (per XPRIZE)
Finals testing June 15-28, 2026 at Nenana Municipal Airport, Nenana, Alaska (near Fairbanks) (per XPRIZE)
UAF ACUASI managed the surrounding airspace during the competition (per XPRIZE)
Dr. Catherine Cahill, Director of ACUASI (per XPRIZE)
Dr. Laura Conner, UAF Vice Chancellor for Research (per XPRIZE)
Dryad Silvanet AI sensor network: 30,000+ sensors, 50+ customers (per Dryad)
Silvaguard suppression drone: up to 100 liters of suppressant (per Dryad)
Dryad says it demonstrated autonomous detection + suppression during the June 2026 Alaska finals (per Dryad)
Winner announced September 2026 (per Dryad, judging pending)

QUOTE (verbatim): 'Detecting fires within minutes and extinguishing them before they become catastrophic is no longer a vision for the future. It is a reality.' — Carsten Brinkschulte, CEO and Co-Founder, Dryad Networks (per Business Wire)
must_hedge_or_cut: Frame Dryad performance as its OWN claim ('Dryad says/demonstrated'), NOT a confirmed win. Judging pending Sept 2026.
'3 finalists' only WITH 'in the Autonomous Wildfire Response track' (separate Space-Based Detection track exists).
ACUASI = 'managed the airspace', not 'ran the range'. Nenana/ACUASI/UAF/Cahill/Conner attributed to XPRIZE, not Dryad.
Nenana = 'near Fairbanks' (no specific drive time). Cut Must Read Alaska (404). Cut VOC/CO/PM2.5 chemistry (unverified).
A controlled airfield test is NOT a real megafire in roadless boreal forest; do not imply proven at scale.
Finalists include a defense prime (Anduril): dual-use tech.
